Name: Aggron
Effects: Search your discard pile for all Energy cards and show them to your opponent. If you find any [M] Special Energy cards there this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage. Put all of those Energy cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may do 60 damage plus 40 more damage. If you do, Aggron does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: While seeking iron for food, it digs tunnels by breaking through bedrock with its steel horns.

Name: Alakazam
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your opponent’s turn, when your opponent’s Pokémon uses any Poké-Power, you may discard 2 cards from your hand and prevent all effects of that Poké-Power. (This counts as that Pokémon using its Poké-Power.) This power can’t be used if Alakazam is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Alakazam by attacks from your opponent’s Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its superb memory lets it recall everything it has experienced from birth. Its IQ exceeds 5,000.

Name: Ambipom
Effects: Your opponent flips a coin until he or she gets heads. For each tails, remove an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon and put it on the bottom of your opponent’s deck.
Before Ambipom does damage, your opponent may discard 2 cards from his or her hand. If he or she does, this attack’s base damage is 10 instead of 80.
Flavor-Text: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.

Name: Azelf
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have Uxie and Mesprit in play, the attack cost of each of your opponent’s Basic Pokémon’s attacks is [C] more. You can’t use more than 1 Downer Material Poké-Body each turn.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, your opponent can’t attach any Special Energy cards from his or her hand to any of his or her Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Willpower.” It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.

Name: Blissey
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Happiny is anywhere under Blissey, you may choose a number of cards in your hand up to the amount of Energy attached to Blissey and put those cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. Then, draw that many cards. This power can’t be used if Blissey is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 for each Energy attached to Blissey. Before doing damage, you may search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Blissey.
Flavor-Text: This kindhearted Pokémon nurses sick Pokémon to health. It senses feelings of sadness.

Name: Bronzong
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card. Then, discard a card from your hand. If you discard an Energy card, draw 1 more card. This power can’t be used if Bronzong is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: One caused a news sensation when it was dug up at a construction site after a 2,000-year sleep.

Name: Celebi
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to Celebi. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may move any number of basic [G] Energy cards attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It has the power to travel across time, but is is said to appear only in peaceful times.

Name: Feraligatr
Effects: Choose as many Energy cards from your hand as you like and show them to your opponent. This attack does 20 damage times the number of Energy cards you chose. Put those Energy cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking and discard it.
Flavor-Text: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

Name: Garchomp
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If an Active Pokémon has Weakness to any of the types of Energy attached to Garchomp, Garchomp’s attacks do 40 more damage to that Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Rainbow Scale Poké-Body can’t be used if Garchomp has any Special Energy cards attached to it.
Flavor-Text: When it folds up its body and extends its wings, it looks like a jet plane. It flies at sonic speed.

Name: Honchkrow
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Honchkrow has the Energy necessary to use its attack, each of your Murkrow can use Honchkrow’s attack as its own without the Energy necessary to use that attack.
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: Becoming active at night, it is known to swarm with numerous MURKROW in tow.

Name: Lumineon
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Lumineon is your Active Pokémon, you may choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has a maximum HP of 100 or more and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Lumineon is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Lumineon. Then, return all [W] Energy attached to Lumineon to your hand.
Flavor-Text: It lives on the deep-sea floor. It attracts prey by flashing the patterns on its four tail fins.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
If Magby isn’t anywhere under Magmortar, discard 2 Energy cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 Energy cards from your hand, this attack does nothing.)
Flavor-Text: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees F from the ends of its arms. It lives in volcanic craters.

Name: Meganium
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If the first coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If the second coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. If the third coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If your opponent has any [R] Pokémon in play, this attack does 60 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its breath has the fantastic ability to revive dead plants and flowers.

Name: Mesprit
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have Uxie and Azelf in play, the Retreat Cost for each Uxie, Mesprit, and Azelf (both yours and your opponent’s) is 0.
Effects: You may switch Mesprit with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [L] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard all basic [L] Energy cards attached to Raichu. This attack does 30 damage times the number of [L] Energy cards you discarded.
Flavor-Text: It turns aggressive if it has too much electricity in its body. It discharges power through its tail.

Name: Typhlosion
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Typhlosion is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard a [W] Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It attacks using blasts of fire. It creates heat shimmers with intense fire to hide itself.

Name: Tyranitar
Effects: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage and discard the top 3 cards from your opponent’s deck.
Each player discards the top card of his or her deck. This attack does 80 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: If it rampages, it knocks down mountains and buries rivers. Maps must be redrawn afterward.

Name: Uxie
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your opponent’s turn, if Uxie is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Uxie is Knocked Out), you may use this power. The Attacking Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Effects: If you have Mesprit and Azelf in play, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Being of Knowledge.” It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

Name: Abomasnow
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Abomasnow is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Abomasnow is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Asleep.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It whips up blizzards in mountains that are always buried in snow. It is the abominable snowman.

Name: Ariados
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: The Retreat Cost for each player’s Pokémon (excluding Ariados) is [C] more.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It attaches silk to its prey and sets it free. Later, it tracks the silk to the prey and its friends.

Name: Bastiodon
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by your opponent’s attacks.
Effects: If Bastiodon was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Any frontal attack is repulsed. It is a docile Pokémon that feeds on grass and berries.

Name: Chimecho
Effects: Draw a card. If Chingling is anywhere under Chimecho, draw 2 more cards.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, that Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: To knock foes flying, it makes the air shudder with its cries. It converses using seven cries.

Name: Crobat
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Crobat from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Flip a coin. If tails, shuffle Crobat and all cards attached to it back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Having four wings enables it to fly faster and more quietly. It turns active when the night comes.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Flip a coin for each basic Energy card attached to Exeggutor and to the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Search your deck for up to 2 [G] Energy cards and attach them to any of your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It is called “The Walking Jungle.” If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an EXEGGCUTE.

Name: Glalie
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Glalie is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by attacks from your opponent’s Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t play any Trainer cards or Supporter cards from his or her hand during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: To protect itself, it clads its body in an armor of ice, made by freezing moisture in the air.

Name: Gyarados
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Gyarados can use any attack from its Basic Pokémon. (You still have to pay for that attack’s Energy cost.) If Gyarados uses any attack from its Basic Pokémon, that attack does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking and discard it. If the first coin is tails, Gyarados is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Once it appears, its rage never settles until it has razed the fields and mountains around it.

Name: Kricketune
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Kricketot and each Kricketune you have in play.
Flavor-Text: It crosses its knifelike arms in front of its chest when it cries. It can compose melodies ad lib.

Name: Manectric
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of basic Energy cards attached to Manectric.
Does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that is the same type as the Defending Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

Name: Mantine
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Mantyke is anywhere under Mantine, the Retreat Cost for each of your [W] Pokémon is [C][C] less.
Effects: Mantine can’t use Giant Wave during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: When the waves are calm, one may encounter a swarm of Mantine swimming as if they are in flight.

Name: Mr. Mime
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon that has 2 or less Energy cards attached to it attacks Mr. Mime, prevent all damage done to Mr. Mime from that attack. If Mime Jr. is anywhere under Mr. Mime, prevent all effects of that attack, including damage, done to Mr. Mime.
Effects: Put a coin next to your Active Pokémon without showing your opponent and cover it with your hand. Your opponent guesses if the coin is heads or tails. If he or she is wrong, this attack does 50 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If he or she is right, Mr. Mime does 20 damage to itself, and this attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It is a pantomime expert that can create invisible but solid walls using miming gestures.

Name: Nidoqueen
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: The attack cost of your Nidoran Female, Nidorina, Nidoran Male, Nidorino, and Nidoking’s attacks is [C] less.
Effects: If you have the same number of or less Benched Pokémon than your opponent, this attack’s base damage is 50 instead of 90.
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is armored with hard scales. It will protect the young in its burrow with its life.

Name: Ninetales
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Ninetales is the same type as that Pokémon (all if that Pokémon is more than 1 type) until the end of your turn. This power can’t be used if Ninetales is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to Ninetales.
Flavor-Text: Its nine tails are said to be imbued with a mystic power. It can live for a thousand years.

Name: Rampardos
Effects: As long as the Defending Pokémon’s remaining HP is 60 or less, this attack’s base damage is 60 instead of 30.
Rampardos does 20 damage to itself. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its powerful head butt has enough power to shatter even the most durable things upon impact.

Name: Slaking
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Slaking is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, Slaking’s Lazy Blow attack’s base damage is 130 during this turn. If tails, Slaking can’t attack or retreat during this turn. (If Slaking is no longer your Active Pokémon, this effect ends.)
Flavor-Text: The world’s laziest Pokémon. When it is lounging, it is actually saving energy for striking back.

Name: Sudowoodo
Effects: If Bonsly is anywhere under Sudowoodo, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Sudowoodo during your opponent’s next turn.
Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Despite appearing to be a tree, its body is closer to rocks and stones. It is very weak to water.

Name: Toxicroak
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Your opponent can’t remove the Special Condition Poisoned by evolving or devolving his or her Poisoned Pokémon. (This also includes putting a Pokémon Level-Up card onto the Poisoned Pokémon.)
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

Name: Unown I
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown I, Unown T, Unown E, and Unown M on your Bench, you may search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can’t use more than 1 ITEM Poké-Power each turn.
Effects: Choose an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon and put it face down. Treat that card as a Special Energy card that provides [C] Energy and doesn’t have any effect other than providing Energy. Put that card face up at the end of your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Ursaring
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused. During your opponent’s next turn, that Pokémon’s attacks do 60 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

If Teddiursa is on your Bench, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage. If Teddiursa is on your Bench and has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 damage plus 40 more damage and remove all damage counters from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In its territory, it leaves scratches on trees that bear delicious berries or fruits.

Name: Walrein
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Walrein from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may flip 2 coins. If both are heads, discard 1 of the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it. (This doesn’t count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)
Effects: If your opponent doesn’t discard a card from his or her hand, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.

Name: Whiscash
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on Whiscash.
Does 20 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is very territorial. It repels foes by setting of tremors that extend over a three-mile radius.

Name: Bayleef
Effects: Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The buds that ring its neck give off a spicy aroma that perks people up.

Name: Chingling
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Chimecho from your hand onto Chingling (this counts as evolving Chingling) and remove all damage counters from Chingling.
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It emits cries by agitating an orb at the back of its throat. It moves with flouncing hops.

Name: Cranidos
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its skull is as hard as iron.

Name: Croconaw
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Croconaw from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose all Energy cards you find there, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Once it bites down, it won’t let go until it loses its fangs. New fangs quickly grow into place.

Name: Dewgong
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Dewgong is Asleep, any damage done to Dewgong by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Dewgong is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: In snow, the pure white coat covering its body obscures it from predators.

Name: Dodrio
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Choose 3 of your opponent’s Evolved Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: When DODUO evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.

Name: Dunsparce
Effects: Draw a card.
Switch Dunsparce with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It digs into the ground with its tail and makes a mazelike nest. It can fly just a little.

Name: Gabite
Effects: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.
Flip a coin. If heads put 4 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If tails, remove 4 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There is a long-held belief that medicine made from its scale will heal even incurable illnesses.

Name: Girafarig
Effects: Search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its tail also has a small brain. It bites to repel any foe trying to sneak up on it from behind.

Name: Golbat
Effects: Look at your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It loves the blood of humans and Pokémon. It flies around at night in search of neck veins.

Name: Graveler
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: GRAVELER make their homes on sheer cliff faces by gouging out numerous horizontal holes.

Name: Happiny
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Chansey from your hand onto Happiny (this counts as evolving Happiny) and remove all damage counters from Happiny.
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It loves round white things. It carries and egg-shaped rock in imitation of CHANSEY.

Name: Lairon
Effects: Search your discard pile for a [M] Energy card and attach it to Lairon. If you do, remove 2 damage counters from Lairon.
Flavor-Text: For food, it digs up iron ore. It smashes its steely body against others to fight over territory.

Name: Magmar
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to Magmar.
Flavor-Text: Born in the spout of a volcano, its body is covered by flames that shimmer like the sun.

Name: Masquerain
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of [C] Energy in the Defending Pokémon’s Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).
If your opponent has any [W] Pokémon in play, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.

Name: Nidorina
Effects: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Pokémon, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.
Flavor-Text: When it senses danger, it raises all the barbs on its body. These barbs grow slower than NIDORINO’s.

Name: Octillery
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon has any Special Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage. Then, discard a Special Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It lives in the gaps of boulders and in holes on the seafloor. Its suction cups grip prey tightly.

Name: Parasect
Effects: If Parasect evolved from Paras during this turn, this attack does 40 damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
Remove 2 damage counters from Parasect.
Flavor-Text: A mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls PARASECT. It scatters poisonous spores.

Name: Pupitar
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Pupitar does 10 damage to itself. Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Pupitar by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its body is as hard as bedrock. By venting pressurized gas, it can launch itself like a rocket.

Name: Quilava
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to Quilava.
Flavor-Text: It intimidates foes with the heat of its flames. The fire burn more strongly when it readies to fight.

Name: Sandslash
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if Sandslash is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Sandslash is Knocked Out), put 4 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, that Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It curls up, then rolls into foes with its back. Its sharp spines inflict severe damage.

Name: Sealeo
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It habitually spins things on its nose. By doing so, it learns textures and odors.

Name: Shieldon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Shieldon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its facial hide is extremely hard.

Name: Tropius
Effects: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Benched Pokémon that has any [G] Energy attached to it.
Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Because it continually ate only its favorite fruit, the fruit started growing around its neck.

Name: Unown E
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown E is on your Bench, you may discard all cards attached to Unown E and attach Unown E to 1 of your Pokémon as a Pokémon Tool card. As long as Unown E is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon gets +10 HP.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, whenever your opponent flips a coin, treat it as tails.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Unown M
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown M is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Unown M is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage. If tails, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon, and this attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Unown T
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown T is your Active Pokémon, you may discard a card from your hand. Then, flip a coin. If heads, put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Unown T is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Look at your opponent’s hand and choose 1 card, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck. Then, show your opponent your hand and he or she chooses 1 card. Shuffle that card into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Vigoroth
Effects: If Vigoroth evolved from Slakoth during this turn and Slakoth was Asleep, this attack’s base damage is 60 instead of 10.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its heart beats at a tenfold tempo, so it cannot sit still even for a moment.

Name: Abra
Effects: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other 4 cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Search your deck for Alakazam and put it on Abra (this counts as evolving Abra). Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It sleeps for 18 hours a day. Even when awake, it teleports itself while remaining seated.

Name: Aipom
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses its tail to pluck fruits that are out of reach. Its tail is more adept than its real hands.

Name: Aron
Effects: Aron does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It usually lives deep in mountains. However, hunger may drive it to eat railroad tracks and cars.

Name: Barboach
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It coats its entire body with a slimy fluid so it can squirm and slip away if grabbed.

Name: Bidoof
Held-Item-Effect: Any damage done to Bidoof by attacks from [L] Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

Name: Buizel
Held-Item-Effect: If Buizel is Asleep, remove the Special Condition Asleep from Buizel at the end of each player’s turn.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Chansey
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Chansey by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Chansey does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is said to deliver happiness. Being compassionate, it shares its eggs with injured people.

Name: Chikorita
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.

Name: Croagunk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t play any Supporter cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its cheeks hold poison sacs. It tries to catch foes off guard to jab them with toxic fingers.

Name: Cyndaquil
Flavor-Text: It has a timid nature. If it is startled, the flames on its back burn more vigorously.

Name: Doduo
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Doduo can’t use Accelerating Stab during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t attach any Energy cards from his or her hand to the Active Pokémon during his or her next turn.
Flavor-Text: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to Exeggcute. Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. For each heads, this attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.

Name: Finneon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Finneon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: After long exposure to sunlight, the patterns on its tail fins shine vividly when darkness arrives.

Name: Geodude
Effects: Choose 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Many live on mountain trails and remain half buried while keeping an eye on climbers.

Name: Gible
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It nests in small, horizontal holes in cave walls. It pounces to catch prey that stray too close.

Name: Kricketot
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It shakes its head back to front, causing its antennae to hit each other and sound like a xylophone.

Name: Larvitar
Effects: You may discard the top card of your deck. If you do, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage and Larvitar is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that eats soil. Once it has eaten a large mountain, it goes to sleep so it can grow.

Name: Magby
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Magmar from your hand onto Magby (this counts as evolving Magby) and remove all damage counters from Magby.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Its body temperature is around 1,100 degrees F. It is healthy if it is breathing yellow flames.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Magikarp.
Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be the world’s weakest Pokémon. No one knows why it has managed to survive.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Search your deck for Murkrow and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Switch Murkrow with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is believed that seeing this Pokémon at night will bring about ominous occurrences.

Name: Nidoran Female
Effects: Search your deck for Nidoran Male or Nidoran Female and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: While it does not prefer to fight, even one drop of the poison it secretes from barbs can be fatal.

Name: Paras
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host PARAS.

Name: Pichu
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Pikachu from your hand onto Pichu (this counts as evolving Pichu) and remove all damage counters from Pichu.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The electric pouches on its cheeks are still small. They cannot store much electricity yet.

Name: Pikachu
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Pichu is anywhere under Pikachu, you may search your discard pile for a [L] Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. This power can’t be used if Pikachu is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in forests with others. It stores electricity in the pouches on its cheeks.

Name: Remoraid
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It squirts water forcefully from its mouth to shoot down flying prey.

Name: Sandshrew
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: To protect itself from attackers, it curls up into a ball. It lives in arid regions with minimal rainfall.

Name: Seel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that lives on icebergs. It swims in the sea using the point on its head to break up ice.

Name: Shinx
Held-Item-Effect: If Shinx is Burned, remove the Special Condition Burned from Shinx at the end of each player’s turn.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a [L] Energy card and attach it to Shinx.

Name: Slakoth
Effects: Both Slakoth and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It spends nearly all its time in a day sprawled out. Just seeing it makes one drowsy.

Name: Snorunt
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: In the snow country, certain folklore says a house will prosper if a SNORUNT lives there.

Name: Snover
Effects: Look at your opponent’s hand.
Flip a coin. If tails, Snover does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It lives on snowy mountains. Having had little contact with humans, it is boldly inquisitive.

Name: Spheal
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your next turn, Spheal’s Rollout attack’s base damage is 40.
Flavor-Text: It rolls across ice floes to reach shore because its body is poorly shaped for swimming.

Name: Spinarak
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It sets a trap by spinning a web with thin but strong silk. It waits motionlessly for prey to arrive.

Name: Surskit
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.

Name: Teddiursa
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions and 2 damage counters from Teddiursa. Teddiursa is now Asleep.
Before doing damage, remove 1 damage counter from the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lets honey soak into its paws so it can lick them all the time. Every sets of paws tastes unique.

Name: Totodile
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has the habit of biting anything with its developed jaws. Even its Trainer need to be careful.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It controls balls of fire. As it grows, its six tails split from their tips to make more tails.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Even though it has no eyes, it can sense obstacles using ultrasonic waves it emits from its mouth.

Name: Bebe’s Search
Description: Choose a card from your hand and put it on top of your deck. Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. (If this is the only card in your hand, you can’t play this card.)
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Dusk Ball
Description: Look at the 7 cards from the bottom of your deck. Choose 1 Pokémon you find there, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Put the remaining cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Fossil Excavator
Description: Search your deck or discard pile for a Trainer card that has Fossil in its name or a Stage 1 or Stage 2 Evolution card that evolves from a Fossil. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. If you searched your deck, shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Lake Boundary
Description: Apply Weakness for each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) as ×2 instead.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Night Maintenance
Description: Search your discard pile for up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

Name: Quick Ball
Description: Reveal cards from your deck until you reveal a Pokémon. Show that Pokémon to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other revealed cards back into your deck. (If you don’t reveal a Pokémon, shuffle all the revealed cards back into your deck.)

Name: Team Galactic’s Wager
Description: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, and you and your opponent play “Rock-Paper-Scissors.” The player who wins draws up to 6 cards. The player who loses draws up to 3 cards. (You draw your cards first.)
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Armor Fossil
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Whenever Armor Fossil would be damaged by your opponent’s attack, flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, reduce that damage by 10.
Description: Play Armor Fossil as if it were a [C] Basic Pokémon. (Armor Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Armor Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Armor Fossil can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Armor Fossil from play. (This doesn’t count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

Name: Skull Fossil
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: During your opponent’s turn, if Skull Fossil would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, put 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.
Description: Play Skull Fossil as if it were a [C] Basic Pokémon. (Skull Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Skull Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Skull Fossil can’t be affected by any Special Conditions and can’t retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Skull Fossil from play. (This doesn’t count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

Name: Multi Energy
Description: Attach Multi Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Multi Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.) Multi Energy provides [C] Energy when attached to a Pokémon that already has Special Energy cards attached to it.

Name: Darkness Energy
Description: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Darkness Energy is attached to isn’t [D]. Darkness Energy provides [D] Energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Metal Energy
Description: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn’t [M]. Metal Energy provides [M] Energy. (Doesn’t count as a basic Energy card.)

Name: Electivire LV.X
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Electivire is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: Discard all of your opponent’s Pokémon Tool cards and Stadium cards in play. If you do, prevent all effects, including damage, done to Electivire during your opponent’s next turn.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Electivire. Electivire LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Lucario LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Lucario LV.X from your hand onto your Active Lucario, you may use this power. Prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Lucario during your opponent’s next turn. (If Lucario is no longer your Active Pokémon, this effect ends.)
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Lucario by attacks is increased by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Lucario. Lucario LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Magmortar LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Magmortar is your Active Pokémon, you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now Burned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 2 on that Pokémon between turns. This power can’t be used if Magmortar is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to Magmortar. Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) During your next turn, Magmortar can’t use Flame Bluster.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Magmortar. Magmortar LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Time-Space Distortion
Description: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, search your discard pile for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.
